fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1cc3e49
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification channel
+ * implementations must fulfill.
+ *
+ * Architecture: The NotificationService dispatches notifications through
+ * adapters. Each adapter handles one channel (email, webhook, SMS, push).
+ * Adapters are registered with the NotificationService and selected based
+ * on the channel type.
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - All adapters must implement this interface
+ * - Adapters must be stateless with respect to notification delivery
+ * - Adapters must return a NotificationResult indicating success or failure
+ * - Adapters must not throw exceptions; they must return { success: false, error: ... }
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles. */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this adapter's channel.
+   * Must not throw — return a failed NotificationResult instead.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-h6Bxth.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: architecture choice (event-driven vs direct calls), core types, notification service, email adapter

alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, no event log, inconsistent with existing architecture docs and CLAUDE.md guidelines

alternatives.option: Mixed approach (keep both EventBus and CallbackRegistry)
alternatives.reason_rejected: Explicitly called out as inconsistent in existing code comments. Creates confusion about which pattern to use for new features.

constraints: All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface
All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()
No direct cross-service calls for side effects
Event handlers registered in listener service constructors
Events must carry all data the handler needs
parameters.context: The existing codebase has an EventBus and CallbackRegistry used inconsistently. CLAUDE.md and architecture docs both state event-driven is the preferred pattern. Need to design a foundation that 11 other developers will build on.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is already the stated architectural preference. It provides loose coupling, event logging/replay, and natural extension points for new notification channels. The adapter pattern on top lets us add channels (email, webhook, SMS, push) without modifying the core service. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() -> NotificationService -> NotificationAdapter implementations.
parameters.summary: Unify notification system on event-driven EventBus pattern with pluggable NotificationAdapter interface

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order lifecycle events (creation, status changes) are emitted through
 * EventBus. The NotificationService and other listeners subscribe independently.
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */

parameters.detail:
Implemented notification system foundation with these components:

1. **Architecture**: Unified on event-driven EventBus pattern (removed CallbackRegistry usage from OrderService)
2. **Core types** (`src/models/notification.ts`): NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, Notification
3. **Adapter interface** (`src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts`): NotificationAdapter interface that all channel implementations must fulfill
4. **Email adapter** (`src/adapters/email.adapter.ts`): EmailNotificationAdapter stub implementation
5. **Refactored NotificationService** (`src/services/notification.service.ts`): Uses only EventBus for events, dispatches through registered adapters
6. **Updated OrderService**: Status changes now emit via EventBus instead of CallbackRegistry

Constraints for other developers:
- All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface
- All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()
- Register adapters via NotificationService.registerAdapter()
- No direct cross-service calls for side effects
- Events must carry all data the handler needs
parameters.summary: Notification system foundation complete: event-driven EventBus + NotificationAdapter pattern

parameters.detail: The CallbackRegistry utility and its statusChangeCallbacks export are no longer imported by any service. They remain in the codebase but are dead code after the EventBus unification. A future agent may remove them or repurpose them if needed.
parameters.summary: CallbackRegistry still exists in codebase but is no longer used — can be removed in cleanup

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Unify notification system on event-driven EventBus with pluggable adapters

Replace mixed EventBus/CallbackRegistry pattern with a consistent
event-driven architecture. All notifications now flow through EventBus
events and are dispatched via NotificationAdapter implementations.

- Add notification types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.)
- Add NotificationAdapter interface for pluggable delivery channels
- Add EmailNotificationAdapter stub implementation
- Refactor NotificationService to use only EventBus + adapters
- Migrate OrderService status changes from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- Update tests for unified event-driven pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
